title: Add SKTouchCanvasView Blazor library with touch event support
body:
- [x] Create `source/SkiaSharp.Extended.UI.Blazor/` library with touch types only (no gesture view, no SKCanvasView wrapper)
  - [x] SKTouchAction enum, SKTouchDeviceType enum, SKTouchEventArgs class
  - [x] SKTouchCanvasView Razor component + SKTouchInterop.js
  - [x] Project file (net9.0;net10.0) + _Imports.razor + README.md
- [x] Add touch demo page — single canvas with device-colored strokes, action/device counters, event log
- [x] Add unit tests — 15 tests, all passing
- [x] Update solution file, NavMenu, solution filters (Pack/Test) for CI
- [x] Fix Blazor 404 on GitHub Pages by adding `.nojekyll` to gh-pages root (disables Jekyll which strips `_framework/` dirs)

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

💡 You can make Copilot smarter by setting up custom instructions, customizing its development environment and configuring Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Learn more [Copilot coding agent tips](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips) in the docs.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot please make the demo be a single canvas. Maybe we can have different colors for different device types. Also, we can log the action types as well as have counted for each action. 

Also, please maybe record a gif of you interacting with the canvas. I want to see an aesthetic stroke.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please make the demo be a single canvas. Maybe we can have different colors for different device types. Also, we can log the action types as well as have counted for each action. 
> 
> Also, plea...

Consolidated into a single canvas in 284fa9a. Strokes are now colored by device type (blue=Mouse, coral=Touch, green=Stylus), with action counters, device counters, and a live event log below the canvas.

![Touch demo with strokes](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/433b64d3-38e2-4c42-a51c-bd5c71d35c76)

I'm unable to record an animated GIF from this sandboxed environment — you'd need to interact with the running sample at `/touch` to see the live drawing experience.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
@copilot+claude-opus-4.6 blazor fails to load 

> blazor.webassembly.js:1   Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 ()

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 blazor fails to load 
> 
> > blazor.webassembly.js:1   Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 ()

The 404 on `blazor.webassembly.js` was caused by GitHub Pages using Jekyll processing, which strips directories starting with `_` (like `_framework/`). Fixed in ccfbe9c by adding a `.nojekyll` file to the root of the gh-pages branch in all deployment paths (main build, same-repo staging, and fork PR staging). The CI workflow will re-run and redeploy the staging site with this fix.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 clicking/touching and dragging on the canvas does not draw lines.
